title: Error Feed — behaviour review and fix.
note:
Error Feed — behaviour review and fix.

The error feed currently has Investigate / Resolved / Skip buttons but 
the workflow is confusing. Here's how it should actually work:

═══════════════════════════════
CORRECT ERROR FEED WORKFLOW
═══════════════════════════════

1. A cron job fails → error appears in the Error Feed tab with a 
   "NEW" badge

2. I click "Investigate" → 
   - The error status changes to "IN PROGRESS" 
   - A corresponding task is automatically created in the Tasks 
     section under "In Progress" so the crew knows to work on it
   - The error stays visible in the feed with an in-progress indicator
   - The button changes to show "Resolved" and "Skip"

3. The crew fixes the underlying issue. The cron job runs successfully 
   on its next scheduled run →
   - The error automatically clears from the Error Feed
   - The linked task in the Tasks section moves to "Done"
   - No manual "Resolved" click needed — the system verified the fix

4. If I click "Resolved" manually →
   - For when I've verified the fix myself
   - Error disappears from the feed
   - Linked task moves to Done

5. If I click "Skip" →
   - For known non-critical issues I'm choosing to ignore
   - Error disappears from the feed
   - No task is created

6. If the same job fails again later →
   - A NEW error appears (not the old one reopened)
   - Fresh timestamp, fresh status
   - Does NOT resurrect the old skipped/resolved error

═══════════════════════════════
KEY RULES
═══════════════════════════════

- Errors should NOT show "Completed" with an "In Progress" flag 
  at the same time (current bug)
- When a cron job succeeds after being in error state, the error 
  should auto-clear — the system verified the fix
- The "Investigate" button creates a task in the Tasks board so 
  work is tracked
- The error feed reflects REALITY: if the job is still failing, 
  the error stays. If it succeeds, the error goes away.
- The cron job entry in the Scheduler list should show an error 
  badge ONLY while there's an active unresolved error

═══════════════════════════════
WHAT TO FIX NOW
═══════════════════════════════

1. Fix the double-status bug (no more "Completed" + "In Progress" 
   showing simultaneously)
2. Wire Investigate button to auto-create a task in the Tasks board
3. Wire cron job success to auto-clear the corresponding error
4. Wire cron job error badge to auto-clear when error is resolved
5. Confirm Skip behaviour works as described above
6. Test the full flow end-to-end

Report what the current behaviour actually is, then fix it to 
match this spec.  [auto-dispatched 2026-06-01 11:53] [auto-dispatched 2026-06-02 11:59] [auto-dispatched 2026-06-02 12:29] [HEARTBEAT 2026-06-02 12:47: Task has been auto-dispatched 6+ times with zero progress. Setting stalledAt to prevent further re-dispatch. Needs human triage.] [auto-dispatched 2026-06-02 12:59] [auto-dispatched 2026-06-02 14:29]

title: Auto-Incident Creation from System Errors
note: P1/P2 errors in the Error Feed should automatically create an Incident AND a linked Task on the Kanban board. When a cron job fails with a P1/P2 classification, the system creates both records and links them. The incident captures the error context; the task tracks resolution work.

history.details: Backlog planning — created from strategic roadmap

title: Incident → Task Linking
note: When an incident is created, a corresponding task must appear in the Kanban board automatically. Bidirectional linking: incident shows linked tasks, task shows parent incident. Status changes propagate (task done → incident check for auto-resolution).

history.details: Backlog planning — created from strategic roadmap

title: Response Action Lifecycle
note: Implement full lifecycle for incident response actions: NEW → INVESTIGATING → MITIGATING → RESOLVED. Each action tracks assignee, timestamps, and state transitions. Actions are visible in the Incident Details panel and update the incident's overall status.

history.details: Backlog planning — created from strategic roadmap

title: Operational Work Panel
note: Add a filtered view that shows only incident and operational tasks, visually distinct from feature work. Different color coding, separate section in the Kanban, and a toggle to show/hide operational vs. feature work.

history.details: Backlog planning — created from strategic roadmap

title: Awaiting Review Incident Flow
note: Incidents require review before closure — same pattern as task completion but with an explicit 'awaiting review' gate. Once all response actions are resolved, the incident moves to AWAITING_REVIEW. A reviewer must approve closure before it moves to DONE.

history.details: Backlog planning — created from strategic roadmap

title: Runbook Data Model
note: Design and implement a structured data model for runbooks: incident type, severity, response steps (ordered list), estimated time, required roles, success criteria, and escalation path. Runbooks are stored as structured data, not free text.

history.details: Backlog planning — created from strategic roadmap

title: Runbook UI
note: Display runbook steps when viewing an incident. Show the relevant runbook based on incident type, progress through steps, and allow agents to mark steps as complete. Clean, scannable layout — not a wall of text.

history.details: Backlog planning — created from strategic roadmap

title: Incident Type Mapping
note: Classify incidents into types (e.g., cron failure, rate limit, service down, dependency break). Auto-suggest incident type based on error patterns and history. Each type maps to a default runbook and severity.

history.details: Backlog planning — created from strategic roadmap

title: Suggested Action Generation
note: AI suggests response actions based on incident type and resolution history. When an incident is created, the system proposes actions from the matching runbook and similar past incidents. Suggestions appear as pre-filled action cards.

history.details: Backlog planning — created from strategic roadmap

title: Task Creation From Runbooks
note: Convert runbook steps into actionable tasks on the Kanban board. Each step becomes a trackable task with assignee, priority, and due date. Progress on runbook steps is visible both in the incident view and the Kanban.

history.details: Backlog planning — created from strategic roadmap

title: Asset Schema
note: Track systems, services, and agents as first-class assets. Schema: name, type (service/agent/cron/infra), status, owner, health check endpoint, dependencies, last check timestamp. Assets are the things incidents happen TO.

history.details: Backlog planning — created from strategic roadmap

title: Asset View
note: Dedicated view showing all assets and their health status at a glance. Group by type, sort by health, show status indicators. Click an asset to see its incident history, dependencies, and current state.

history.details: Backlog planning — created from strategic roadmap

title: Asset Health Monitoring
note: Automated health checks for each asset. Cron-based or event-driven checks that update asset status (healthy/degraded/down/unknown). Health history is tracked and visible in the asset detail view.

history.details: Backlog planning — created from strategic roadmap

title: Incident-to-Asset Linking
note: Link incidents to affected assets. When creating an incident, select which assets are impacted. Asset view shows active and historical incidents. Incident view shows affected assets with their current health.

history.details: Backlog planning — created from strategic roadmap

title: Dependency Mapping
note: Show relationships between assets: which services depend on which, which agents depend on which services. Visual dependency graph. When an asset goes down, highlight all dependent assets. Critical for understanding blast radius.

history.details: Backlog planning — created from strategic roadmap

title: Unified Event Model
note: All station events (incidents, cron runs, task changes, agent actions) feed into a single event model. Each event has: timestamp, type, source, severity, description, and links to related entities. This is the foundation for the timeline.

history.details: Backlog planning — created from strategic roadmap

title: Incident Events in Timeline
note: Incident lifecycle events (created, status change, action taken, resolved) appear in the unified timeline. Filterable by incident, severity, or time range. Shows the full story of an incident in chronological order.

history.details: Backlog planning — created from strategic roadmap

title: Scheduler Events in Timeline
note: Cron job runs (success, failure, timeout) appear in the timeline. Shows patterns over time — is a job degrading? Failing intermittently? The timeline makes temporal patterns visible. history.details: Backlog planning — created from strategic roadmap
Auto-reset: no activity for >2h (last history entry 3.5h ago)
Re-reset: dispatcher re-dispatched this task after previous stall reset, but no agent picked it up again. Setting dispatchCount=3 to block further auto-dispatches.
Auto-reset: in_progress with no step/progress. Dispatcher-agent handoff gap — task dispatched but no agent to work on it.
3rd re-reset. Dispatcher keeps re-dispatching despite stalledAt. dispatchCount=3 now.

title: Task Events in Timeline
note: Task state changes (created, assigned, started, blocked, completed) appear in the timeline. Shows work flow patterns, bottlenecks, and agent activity over time.

history.details: Backlog planning — created from strategic roadmap

title: Commander Timeline View
note: A dedicated, filterable timeline view showing all station activity. Filter by event type, severity, time range, agent, or asset. This is the 'mission log' — the complete operational history of the station.

history.details: Backlog planning — created from strategic roadmap

title: Automation Health States
note: Monitor the health of each automated process (cron jobs, agents, services). Track: last run status, consecutive failures, average duration, error rate. Health states: healthy, degraded, failing, unknown.

history.details: Backlog planning — created from strategic roadmap

title: Retry Policies with Backoff
note: Configurable retry policies for automated processes. Define max retries, backoff strategy (linear/exponential), and retry conditions. Failed cron jobs automatically retry according to policy before escalating.

history.details: Backlog planning — created from strategic roadmap

title: Circuit Breakers
note: Auto-disable failing automation after threshold breaches. If a cron job fails N times consecutively, it's automatically disabled and an incident is created. Prevents runaway failures and noise. Manual reset required.

history.details: Backlog planning — created from strategic roadmap

title: Provider Cooldown Management
note: Track and manage rate limits across providers (OpenRouter, etc.). When a provider hits rate limits, automatically queue requests, switch to fallback providers, or pause non-critical work. Prevents cascading failures.

history.details: Backlog planning — created from strategic roadmap

title: Auto Remediation
note: Predefined actions for common failure patterns. E.g., if Vite cache corruption is detected → auto-clear cache and restart dev server. If a cron job times out → auto-retry with increased timeout. Configurable remediation rules.

history.details: Backlog planning — created from strategic roadmap

title: Lessons Learned Capture
note: When an incident is resolved, prompt for a brief 'lessons learned' note. What happened? What fixed it? What could prevent it? Stored with the incident and searchable. This is the raw material for operational knowledge.

history.details: Backlog planning — created from strategic roadmap

title: Knowledge Capture Automation
note: Auto-suggest knowledge entries from resolved incidents. AI analyzes the incident + resolution + lessons learned and proposes a structured knowledge base entry. Human reviews and approves before publishing.

history.details: Backlog planning — created from strategic roadmap

title: Runbook Evolution
note: Update runbooks based on resolution history. When an incident is resolved differently than the runbook suggests, flag the runbook for review. Track runbook effectiveness — which runbooks lead to fastest resolution?

history.details: Backlog planning — created from strategic roadmap

title: Framework Sync
note: Keep FRAMEWORK.md in sync with operational learning. When patterns emerge from incidents or knowledge entries, auto-suggest updates to AGENTS.md, TOOLS.md, or FRAMEWORK.md. The station's documentation evolves with its experience.

history.details: Backlog planning — created from strategic roadmap

title: Searchable Knowledge Base
note: A searchable repository of all operational knowledge: lessons learned, runbook updates, incident post-mortems, best practices. Full-text search, tagging, and linking to related incidents and assets.

history.details: Backlog planning — created from strategic roadmap

title: Task Category Badges
note: Add category badges to all tasks: Operations, Infrastructure, Platform, Mission Control, Knowledge, Automation, Security, Technical Debt. Simple colored badge on the Kanban card. Filterable by category. No epics or story points — just a clear label.

history.details: Backlog planning — created from strategic roadmap

title: Priority/Severity System
note: Implement P1 Critical, P2 High, P3 Normal, P4 Background across all work items. Visual hierarchy: P1 gets red/urgent styling, P2 gets orange, P3 gets standard, P4 gets muted. Critical items surface naturally at the top of lists and boards.

history.details: Backlog planning — created from strategic roadmap

title: Temporal Intelligence
note: Stalled task detection (no activity for 2h), aging incident warnings (open for >24h), overdue operations (past expected completion), abandoned work flags. Visual indicators: 'No activity for 2h', 'Awaiting response', 'Escalation threshold approaching'.

history.details: Backlog planning — created from strategic roadmap

title: Agent Accountability
note: Track who owns work, who touched it last, active handoffs, and collaboration relationships. Assigned crew badge on tasks, 'last active by' timestamp, work history/timeline per agent. Clear ownership prevents work from falling through cracks.

history.details: Backlog planning — created from strategic roadmap

title: Incident Response Atmosphere
note: When incidents occur, the station reacts subtly: warning lights in affected rooms, increased monitor activity, alert banners, tense ambient colour shift (slight red/warm tint). Controlled operational awareness — not arcade chaos. Calm but urgent.

history.details: Backlog planning — created from strategic roadmap

title: Queue & Flow Visualisation
note: Dispatch indicators showing work being assigned, moving data signals between stations, subtle hallway traversal animations, queue traffic, 'work arriving' animations. The station communicates that work is flowing through the system — not just sitting in columns.

history.details: Backlog planning — created from strategic roadmap

title: Operational Readiness Dashboard
note: At a glance: what is healthy, what is active, what is blocked, what needs attention, which agents are overloaded. The station communicates system state visually before reading text. Status indicators, health bars, and activity heat maps.

history.details: Backlog planning — created from strategic roadmap
